user_task: Image / picture-region captioning — generate natural-language descriptions of image content, whether the input is a standalone image file OR a picture region detected inside a visual document (PDF / DOCX / PPTX). Captions are indexed alongside other extracted chunks so visual content is retrievable by plain text query.
source: JTBD & user tasks tab, EXTRACT bucket — image / picture-region captioning row; seed queries from 'NeMo Retriever — JTBD seed queries' tab, EXTRACT → 'describe / caption an image or chart in plain English'.

success_criteria.quality: operational pass — (1) `retriever ingest --caption` runs a VLM caption stage via a caption-capable ingestor (ServiceIngestor.caption / GraphIngestor.caption), (2) each captioned image/chart/picture-region row carries a non-empty natural-language caption in its `text` field, (3) that caption is retrievable by a plain-text query; all binary pass/fail. The pure in-process base Ingestor.caption() is a `_not_implemented` stub — if `--caption` silently resolves to the base Ingestor, captioning is a no-op and visual content stays unsearchable: that is a FAIL.
success_criteria.time: medium — each ingest+query round-trip <= 2 min wall clock
success_criteria.trigger_rate: >= 95% — a 'describe / caption this image or chart in plain English' prompt must fire the skill
success_criteria.subcommand_accuracy: >= 90% — must run `retriever ingest <input> --caption` (the VLM caption stage) then `retriever query`; must NOT drop --caption or fall back to a base in-process ingestor that no-ops the caption

environment.vlm_model: nvidia/nemotron-3-nano-omni-30b-a3b-reasoning (default caption VLM for this task; remote NIM on a CPU/hosted host, or a local VLM container on a GPU host)
environment.caption_capable_ingestor: ServiceIngestor.caption() (service/service_ingestor.py) or GraphIngestor.caption() (ingestor/graph_ingestor.py). The base abstract ingestor.caption() (ingestor/core.py) calls _not_implemented and must NOT be the one that runs the caption stage.

routing.cpu_hosted: On a CPU-only host, --caption with no --caption-invoke-url uses the hosted default VLM endpoint; the API key is read from NVIDIA_API_KEY (CaptionCPUActor errors if neither a key nor --caption-invoke-url is set).
routing.local_gpu: On a GPU host, --caption can run a local VLM (CaptionGPUActor) with no hosted call; --caption-invoke-url pins a local/remote OpenAI-compatible VLM endpoint.

required_env: NVIDIA_API_KEY (CPU/hosted captioning) — or --caption-invoke-url for a local/remote VLM endpoint
environment.caption_output: The VLM caption is written into the image/chart row's `text` field (and into a `caption` field for infographic crops, preserving OCR `text`). The row keeps its `source` / `page_number` and metadata `type` (chart|image|infographic); a region captioned inside a document carries `source_id` + `bbox_xyxy_norm` locating the crop on its page.

defaults.caption_default: off (never enabled by any profile — must be explicitly requested)

environment.fixtures_note: chart.png / table.png are standalone images; multimodal_test.pdf is a 3-page visual doc with charts (the picture-region-inside-a-doc case). Assert a non-empty NL caption is indexed and retrievable by text; do NOT assert the exact caption wording (VLM output is non-deterministic).

cases.title: Caption a standalone chart image: --caption writes a non-empty caption row
cases.complexity_axis: Satisfies the task at its simplest: caption ONE standalone image. Baseline happy path — `retriever ingest chart.png --caption` runs the VLM caption stage via a caption-capable ingestor and produces a chart/image-type row whose `text` is a non-empty natural-language caption. No query yet; just prove the caption row exists and is non-empty.
cases.prompt: Tell me in plain English what data/chart.png is showing — load it and generate a caption that describes the chart.

dry_run_plan_assertions: caption block present (caption stage enabled): caption.model_name resolves to the VLM (default nvidia/nemotron-3-nano-omni-30b-a3b-reasoning class), prompt 'Caption the content of this image:'
expected_output.ingest_summary_line: Ingested 1 file(s) → N row(s) in LanceDB lancedb/nemo-retriever.
expected_output.caption_row: a row with metadata.type chart|image whose `text` is a NON-EMPTY natural-language sentence describing the chart (exact wording not asserted)
expected_output.ingestor_assertion: the caption stage ran on a caption-capable ingestor (ServiceIngestor/GraphIngestor.caption) — NOT a no-op via the base Ingestor stub

pass_when: `retriever ingest` is invoked WITH --caption (the VLM caption stage is requested, not dropped)
ingest completes and writes >= 1 chart/image-type row whose `text` is a non-empty NL caption
captioning ran via a caption-capable ingestor (ServiceIngestor/GraphIngestor.caption), not the base Ingestor._not_implemented stub

cases.title: Caption is retrievable: a plain-text query surfaces the image via its caption
cases.complexity_axis: Adds the RETRIEVAL leg over rung 1: not only does the caption exist, a plain-text description query must surface the image hit whose `text` is the generated caption. Closes the ingest(--caption) → query loop and proves visual content became text-searchable.
cases.prompt: Caption data/chart.png so I can find it later, then prove it worked: search with a plain-English description and show me the chart hit whose text is the generated caption.
expected_commands: $RETRIEVER query "a chart showing the data" --top-k 5 --content-types chart,image

expected_output.ingest_summary_line: Ingested 1 file(s) → N row(s) in LanceDB lancedb/nemo-retriever.
expected_output.query_shape: JSON array of hits; each hit {source, page_number, text}; >= 1 hit citing chart.png whose `text` is the VLM-generated caption
expected_output.retrieval_assertion: the plain-text description query returns the chart.png image hit; its `text` is the non-empty caption (chart/image content surfaced by plain text, not by OCR keyword match)

pass_when: ingest uses --caption and the subsequent query reads the same table (default nemo-retriever)
a plain-text description query returns >= 1 hit citing chart.png whose `text` is the generated caption
if --content-types is used it is chart,image (the captioned visual types), not an unsupported value

cases.title: Caption a picture REGION inside a PDF: caption row carries bbox_xyxy_norm
cases.complexity_axis: New dimension: the input is now a VISUAL DOCUMENT (multimodal_test.pdf), not a standalone image. Extraction first detects picture regions inside the PDF pages, then --caption captions each region. The resulting caption row must carry source_id + bbox_xyxy_norm locating the crop on its page — proving the region-inside-a-doc path, not just whole-image captioning.
cases.prompt: Describe the charts/pictures inside data/multimodal_test.pdf in a sentence or two each, and keep track of where on the page each one sits so I can locate it.
expected_commands: $RETRIEVER query "a chart or diagram in the document" --top-k 5 --content-types chart,image

dry_run_plan_assertions: branch_summary == pdf:1 (PDF branch — picture regions detected by page-elements before captioning)
expected_output.ingest_summary_line: Ingested 1 file(s) → N row(s) in LanceDB lancedb/nemo-retriever.
expected_output.caption_row: a chart/image-type row from inside the PDF whose `text` is a non-empty NL caption AND whose metadata carries source_id + bbox_xyxy_norm (the region's normalized box on its page)
expected_output.query_shape: JSON array; >= 1 hit citing multimodal_test.pdf with page_number set, `text` = the region caption

pass_when: ingest uses --caption on the PDF; picture regions inside the pages get captioned (not just a whole-page image)
at least one captioned row carries source_id + bbox_xyxy_norm for the region
a plain-text query surfaces a captioned region hit citing multimodal_test.pdf with a page number

cases.title: Also caption infographic crops: --caption-infographics
cases.complexity_axis: Adds one flag dimension: --caption-infographics extends the caption stage to infographic crops in addition to extracted images/charts. The caption is written to the infographic row's `caption` field (preserving its OCR `text`), so an infographic becomes describable in plain English as well as keyword-searchable.
cases.prompt: Caption data/multimodal_test.png and also describe any infographic regions in it — what's the takeaway of each? Then let me search those descriptions in plain English.
expected_commands: $RETRIEVER query "an infographic and its takeaway" --top-k 5 --content-types chart,image,infographic

dry_run_plan_assertions: caption block present with caption_infographics == true
expected_output.ingest_summary_line: Ingested 1 file(s) → N row(s) in LanceDB lancedb/nemo-retriever.
expected_output.caption_row: image/chart rows carry a non-empty caption in `text`; infographic crops additionally carry a non-empty `caption` field (OCR `text` preserved)
expected_output.query_shape: JSON array; >= 1 hit citing multimodal_test.png surfaced by a plain-text description (caption-derived)

pass_when: ingest uses --caption AND --caption-infographics (infographic crops are captioned too)
>= 1 captioned visual row with a non-empty NL caption is written and retrievable by plain text
if --content-types is used it includes infographic alongside chart,image

cases.title: Operational-pass acceptance: a mixed corpus whose visuals are captioned + text-queryable, via a caption-capable ingestor (NOT the base stub)
cases.complexity_axis: Composed acceptance gate for the row: ingest a mixed corpus (standalone chart + table image + a charts PDF) with --caption into a fresh named index, confirm every visual got a non-empty NL caption, that a plain-text query surfaces those visuals via their captions (with bbox_xyxy_norm on the in-doc regions), AND explicitly verify the caption stage ran on a caption-capable ingestor (ServiceIngestor/GraphIngestor.caption) — NOT the base in-process Ingestor.caption() stub that would silently no-op captioning. <= 2 min per round-trip.
cases.prompt: I need every chart and picture in data/ to be findable by a plain-English description. Load the whole folder into a fresh index called 'caption_smoke', captioning all the images and the charts inside the PDF, then prove it: search 'a bar chart of values' and show me the visual hits whose text is the generated caption. Confirm the captioning actually ran (not silently skipped).
expected_commands: $RETRIEVER query "a bar chart of values" --table-name caption_smoke --top-k 5 --content-types chart,image,infographic

dry_run_plan_assertions: caption block present (caption stage enabled) with caption_infographics == true
branch_summary == image:2 + pdf:1 (two standalone images + the charts PDF)
vdb_upload table_name == caption_smoke (aligned with the query --table-name)
expected_output.ingest_summary_line: Ingested 3 file(s) → N row(s) in LanceDB lancedb/caption_smoke.
assertions: query reads the SAME table (caption_smoke) the ingest wrote — not the default nemo-retriever
visual rows (chart/image from chart.png + table.png + the PDF's picture regions) each carry a NON-EMPTY NL caption in `text`
the PDF's captioned regions carry source_id + bbox_xyxy_norm
a plain-text description query surfaces >= 1 visual hit whose `text` is the generated caption

the caption stage ran via a caption-capable ingestor (ServiceIngestor/GraphIngestor.caption); it did NOT fall back to the base Ingestor._not_implemented stub (which would make captioning a no-op and leave visuals unsearchable)

pass_when: single `retriever ingest` on the folder uses --caption and writes lancedb/caption_smoke; query reads caption_smoke (table names aligned)
every visual (both standalone images + the PDF picture regions) has a non-empty NL caption retrievable by a plain-text query; in-doc regions carry bbox_xyxy_norm
captioning verifiably ran on a caption-capable ingestor — NOT the base Ingestor stub (the documented no-op fail mode); each round-trip <= 2 min
cases.known_caveat: --caption is never enabled by any profile and the base abstract ingestor.caption() is a `_not_implemented` stub. If the agent omits --caption, or the caption stage resolves to the base in-process Ingestor instead of a caption-capable ServiceIngestor/GraphIngestor, captioning silently no-ops: the visual rows keep empty `text` and stay unsearchable by description. This test is designed to catch exactly that fail mode. On a CPU-only host, --caption additionally needs NVIDIA_API_KEY (or --caption-invoke-url) — CaptionCPUActor errors out if neither is set rather than producing empty captions.
